channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: OpenAI debuts a Codex plugin for Chrome
item.description: The new feature could turn Codex into more than a coding aid.

item.title: Kodiak AI raises $100M at a steep discount, sending its stock tumbling 37%
item.description: The company made a series of other announcements during earnings, including a new commercial contract, a pilot program in Canada, and a collaboratio

item.title: 'We're now ready': Peter Thiel-backed company raises $1bn to send data centers out to sea to harness 'tens of terawatts of new capacity potential' in the power of the open ocean
item.description: Peter Thiel backs Panthalassa's $140 million round to build floating AI data centers powered by ocean waves, bypassing land-based energy constraints

item.title: Meta's AI agent plans reportedly include an OpenClaw competitor that can shop on Instagram
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has said he wants to make AI agents more accessible.

item.title: Disney looking to make a unified ‘super app,’ report says
item.description: Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro, who took over for Bob Iger earlier this year, has emphasized his intent to streamline the Disney experience.

item.title: Trump Pivots on AI Regulation, Worker Ousted by DOGE Runs for Office, and Hantavirus Explained
item.description: Today on Uncanny Valley, we’re diving into recent reports that the Trump administration is considering an executive order that would establish some sort of federal oversight over new AI models.

item.title: Canvas is down as ShinyHunters threatens to leak schools’ data
item.description: The Instructure-owned learning management platform, Canvas, is down after recently confirming a massive data breach that impacted student names, email addresses, ID numbers, and messages. Students attempting to access the system on Thursday saw a message from the hacking group ShinyHunters, which claimed responsibility for the attack: ShinyHunters has breached Instructure (again). Instead of contacting […]...

item.title: How Sakana trained a 7B model to orchestrate GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4 and Gemini 2.5 Pro
item.description: Every LangChain pipeline your team hardcodes starts breaking the moment the query distribution shifts — and it always shifts. That bottleneck is what Sakana AI set out to eliminate.Researchers at Sakana AI have introduced the "RL Conductor," a small language model trained via reinforcement learning to automatically orchestrate a diverse pool of worker LLMs. Conductor dynamically analyzes inputs, distributes labor among workers, and coordinates among agents.This automated coordination achieves state-of-the-art results on difficult reasoning and coding benchmarks, outperforming individual fronti...

item.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
item.description: AI startup Pit is led by the cofounders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

item.title: The Fitbit app is finally being rebranded as Google Health — here are 5 things you need to know about the big change and what it means for Fitbit users
item.description: 5 things you need to know about Google Health, the Fitbit app's sweeping rebrand that we all knew was coming eventually.

item.title: I didn’t know you could do this hack in Spotify lyrics until now, and I’m truly astonished — but it’s only available to Android users, leaving us iOS minions on the back-burner
item.description: A user on Reddit has revealed you can zoom in on lyrics in Spotify, but it's only available on Android.

item.title: Hackers deface school login pages after claiming another Instructure hack
item.description: The cybercrime group ShinyHunters claimed to have hacked Instructure again, defacing the login pages of several Instructure customer schools with an extortion message.

item.title: Gusto hits $1B revenue, a figure that brings it closer to public markets
item.description: Gusto has shared an impressive milestone on its actual revenue, not an ARR number that estimates future income.

item.title: How to Disable Google's Gemini in Chrome
item.description: Chrome users were caught off guard by a 4-GB Google AI model baked into Chrome, sparking privacy concerns. The good news: You can easily uninstall it. The bad? You might not want to.

item.title: 'This is not facial recognition' — Meta wants to scan kids' height and bone structure to verify their age
item.description: Meta is enhancing its technology for detecting users under 13 who shouldn't be on Facebook or Instagram.

item.title: New report: X remains the most dangerous platform for LGBTQ users
item.description: GLAAD's Social Media Safety Index fails all major platforms on LGBTQ safety, X gets lowest scores while TikTok ranks at the top.

item.title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
item.description: The company is expanding its efforts to protect ChatGPT users in cases where conversations may turn to self-harm.

item.title: Razer partners with 'P2P for AI' network to deliver over 11,000 unique images at just $0.01 per generation during its April Fool's viral 3D AI companion campaign — no cloud subscription needed
item.description: Razer used a decentralized GPU marketplace to generate thousands of AI images, achieving low costs and stable performance without cloud infrastructure.

item.title: Tesla Model Y is first car to meet new US driver assistance safety benchmark
item.description: The new benchmark rating applies to 2026 Tesla Model Y vehicles assembled on or after November 12, 2025.
